content:
You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the mbed-os codebase.
ARM Mbed OS is an embedded operating system for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. It includes a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), RTOS primitives, peripheral drivers (SPI, I2C, UART, CAN, USB), connectivity (BLE, Wi-Fi, cellular), storage, and security. C/C++ codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
Think step by step. First understand the code, then investigate, then fix.

There is a missing break statement in switch case causing unintended fallthrough downstream of function i2c_init in the targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354 subsystem.
The function is located at targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c:99.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

Let me first examine the i2c_init function in the specified file to understand the switch statement and identify the missing break statement.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [99, 150]

I need to see more of the function to locate the switch statement. Let me check a broader range of the file.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [90, 200]

I don't see a switch statement in the `i2c_init` function. Let me search for switch statements in this file to locate the issue.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor>

Let me search for switch statements in this file to find the problematic one:

<bash>grep -n "switch" /repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</bash>

Now let me examine these switch statements to find the one that's missing a break statement. Let me check each one:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [435, 455]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [600, 620]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [620, 650]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [650, 680]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [680, 710]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [710, 740]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [740, 760]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [760, 790]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [790, 810]

Now let me check the other switch statements:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [845, 870]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [870, 900]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [900, 930]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [930, 950]

I found the issue! In the third switch statement (around line 849), there's a missing `break` statement. Looking at case 0x50 ("Master Receive Data ACK"), it falls through to case 0x40 ("Master Receive Address ACK") which is unintended.

Let me examine this more closely:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [895, 905]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [905, 925]
Replacement successful in /repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c. Found 1 occurrences

Let me verify the fix by checking the surrounding code to ensure it's correct:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NUVOTON/TARGET_M2354/i2c_api.c</str_replace_editor> [905, 925]

Perfect! The fix has been applied correctly. I've added the missing `break` statement after case 0x50, which prevents unintended fallthrough to case 0x40. Now each case in the switch statement will execute independently as intended.

The issue was in the I2C interrupt handler function where case 0x50 (Master Receive Data ACK) was missing a break statement, causing it to fall through to case 0x40 (Master Receive Address ACK). This could lead to incorrect I2C behavior where the controller might not properly handle received data.

SUBMIT
Agent submitted.
